sources.title: Certain Bulk Drug Substances for Use in Compounding that May Present Significant Safety Risks
Multifunctionality and Possible Medical Application of the BPC 157 Peptide—Literature and Patent Review

claims.statement: FDA lists BPC-157 among bulk drug substances that may present significant safety risks for compounding, citing immunogenicity risk for certain routes, peptide-impurity/API-characterization complexity, and limited route-specific safety information.

context.population: humans exposed to compounded BPC-157 products

extractedEvidence.quote: Compounded drugs containing BPC-157 may pose risk for immunogenicity... peptide-related impurities and active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) characterization... limited, safety-related information

reviewFlags: do not present compounded or gray-market products as equivalent to approved drugs

claims.statement: A literature review describes broad preclinical interest in BPC-157 but emphasizes scarce human studies and the need for human-oriented studies to establish therapeutic and toxic profiles.

context.population: human use claims extrapolated from preclinical literature

extractedEvidence.quote: Considering the scarcity of human studies... more extensive human-oriented studies indicating therapeutic and toxic profiles of BPC 157 are required

reviewFlags: do not infer human efficacy from animal models
vendor healing claims require misinformation monitoring

claims.statement: Online claims commonly present BPC-157 as a proven systemic healing peptide; BioStack should label such claims as unsupported unless tied to high-quality human clinical evidence and product-specific safety review.

extractedEvidence.pageOrSection: Review discussion of human evidence limits and accessibility

reviewFlags: do not publish as factual benefit claim

reviewReasons: Pilot misinformation-heavy evidence packet requires human review before publication.
